data.q: What is the role of PHP-FPM in the PHP ecosystem?
o: It is a web server like Apache or Nginx
It is a FastCGI process manager for PHP to handle requests efficiently
It is a PHP code debugger
It is an IDE for PHP development
data.e: PHP-FPM (FastCGI Process Manager) manages PHP processes to handle web requests efficiently, often used with web servers.

data.q: Which of these is the correct order of the PHP-FPM worker lifecycle stages?
o: Accept request > Init > Execute PHP > Terminate
Init > Wait for request > Execute PHP > Terminate
Execute PHP > Init > Accept request > Terminate
Accept request > Execute PHP > Init > Terminate
data.e: PHP-FPM workers initialize, wait to accept requests, execute PHP code, then terminate or recycle.

data.q: What triggers the PHP-FPM worker process to terminate or respawn?
o: When a worker reaches max_requests limit
data.e: PHP-FPM workers are respawned after reaching max_requests to free resources and avoid memory leaks.

data.q: In PHP-FPM, what is the purpose of the master process?
data.e: The master process manages workers and handles signals but doesn't process requests itself.

data.q: How does PHP-FPM optimize performance for PHP web applications?
o: By spawning a new PHP process for every request
By keeping persistent worker processes ready to handle multiple requests
By compiling PHP to machine code ahead of time
data.e: Persistent PHP-FPM workers avoid process creation overhead for each request, improving performance.

data.q: What happens during the PHP-FPM worker initialization phase?
o: The worker accepts a new request immediately
The worker loads PHP environment, extensions, and configures settings
data.e: At initialization, the worker sets up the PHP runtime environment before processing requests.

data.q: Which signal is typically used to gracefully reload PHP-FPM configuration?
data.e: Sending SIGHUP to PHP-FPM master prompts a graceful reload of the config and worker processes.

data.q: During PHP-FPM’s request handling, which component actually executes the PHP code?
data.e: Each worker process executes the PHP code when a request is dispatched to it.

data.q: What PHP-FPM configuration parameter controls how many requests a single worker will serve before respawning?
data.e: pm.max_requests limits the number of requests a worker serves, respawning it for stability.

data.q: Why does PHP-FPM use multiple worker processes in its lifecycle design?
o: To parallelize request handling and improve concurrency
To improve security by isolating each request
To reduce memory usage by sharing workers
To compile PHP scripts in parallel
data.e: Multiple workers enable concurrent processing of requests, improving throughput under load.
